Family Law (Divorce, Custody): What is your full legal name?
What is your spouse's or partner's full legal name?
What is your current marital status and how long have you been married?
Are there any children involved? If so, what are their names and ages?
What is the current living arrangement for you and your spouse?
Are you seeking a divorce, legal separation, or custody modification?
Is there a prenuptial or postnuptial agreement in place?
Are there any existing court orders related to custody, support, or protection?
Do you have any concerns about domestic violence or safety?
What are the primary assets you share — home, vehicles, bank accounts, retirement?
Do both parties have employment income? What are the approximate amounts?
Are there any debts or liabilities that are shared between you and your spouse?
What custody arrangement are you hoping to achieve?
Has either party already filed any paperwork with the court?
Are there any pending issues with child support or spousal support?
Do the children have any special needs that affect custody considerations?
Have you or your spouse consulted with another attorney about this matter?
Are there any restraining orders or orders of protection currently active?
What is your preferred method of resolution — mediation, negotiation, or litigation?
Is there anything else about your family situation that the attorney should know?
What is your timeline — is there any urgency or upcoming court dates?
Do you have access to financial records, tax returns, and bank statements?
Are there any issues related to relocation or moving out of state?
What is the best phone number and email to reach you confidentially?
How did you hear about our firm?
Criminal Defense: What is your full legal name and date of birth?
What are the specific charges you are facing?
When and where did the alleged incident occur?
Were you arrested? If so, when and by which agency?
Are you currently in custody, out on bail, or released on your own recognizance?
What is the bail amount, if applicable?
Do you have an upcoming court date or arraignment? What is the date?
Have you spoken to any law enforcement officers about this matter?
Did you make any statements to the police — written or verbal?
Were there any witnesses to the alleged incident?
Is there any physical evidence involved — video, photos, or documents?
Do you have a prior criminal record or any past convictions?
Are there any pending charges in other jurisdictions?
Have you been charged with a misdemeanor or a felony?
Were any weapons allegedly involved in the incident?
Are there any alleged victims? Do you know their names?
Do you have a copy of the police report or arrest report?
Have you been contacted by a probation or parole officer?
Are you currently on probation or parole for a different case?
Is this a state or federal matter?
Do you have any immigration concerns related to this case?
Have you consulted with or retained any other attorney for this matter?
What is your employment status and occupation?
What is the best way to reach you privately?
Is there anything else about this situation that the attorney should know?
Personal Injury: What is your full legal name and date of birth?
What type of incident occurred — car accident, slip and fall, medical malpractice, workplace injury, or other?
When exactly did the incident happen — date and approximate time?
Where did the incident occur — specific address or location?
Can you describe how the incident happened in your own words?
Who do you believe was at fault for the incident?
Were there any witnesses? Do you have their contact information?
Was a police report or incident report filed?
What injuries did you sustain?
Did you go to the emergency room or hospital? When?
What doctors or medical providers have you seen since the incident?
Are you still receiving medical treatment?
Have you missed any work due to your injuries? How many days?
What is your occupation and approximate annual income?
Do you have health insurance? Has it been covering your treatment?
Do you have auto insurance? What is the coverage type and policy limit?
Do you know if the other party has insurance?
Have you been contacted by any insurance company or adjuster?
Have you given any recorded statements to an insurance company?
Did you take any photos or video at the scene of the incident?
Were there any prior injuries to the same body parts affected?
Have you hired or consulted with any other attorney about this case?
Is there a statute of limitations concern — how long ago did this happen?
What outcome are you hoping for — settlement, compensation amount?
Is there anything else about the incident the attorney should know?
Real Estate: What is your full legal name?
What is the address or location of the property involved?
What type of real estate matter is this — purchase, sale, lease, dispute, or foreclosure?
Are you the buyer, seller, landlord, or tenant in this transaction?
Is this a residential or commercial property?
Are you currently under contract? What is the closing date?
Is this a cash transaction or are you financing through a lender?
Who is the other party involved — name of buyer, seller, or landlord?
Do you have a real estate agent involved? What is their name?
Is there a title company or escrow company already selected?
Are there any title issues, liens, or encumbrances on the property?
Are there any zoning or permit issues you are aware of?
Is there a dispute with a neighbor regarding property boundaries or easements?
Have you received any notices of code violations or HOA violations?
Is there an existing lease agreement? When does it expire?
Are there any environmental concerns or disclosures about the property?
Have you had a property inspection done? Were there any issues found?
Is there a mortgage on the property? What is the outstanding balance?
Are you facing foreclosure or have you received any default notices?
Are there any co-owners or partners involved in the property?
Do you have copies of the deed, contract, or lease agreement?
What is the approximate value of the property?
Have you consulted with another attorney about this matter?
What is your desired outcome or resolution?
Is there any urgency or upcoming deadlines we should know about?
Corporate Law: What is the name of your business or company?
What is the current legal structure — LLC, Corporation, Partnership, or Sole Proprietorship?
In which state is the business registered or incorporated?
What industry does your business operate in?
What specific corporate matter do you need assistance with?
Are you forming a new business or dealing with an existing entity?
How many owners, shareholders, or partners are involved?
Are there any existing operating agreements, bylaws, or partnership agreements?
Are you involved in or considering a merger, acquisition, or sale?
Do you need help drafting or reviewing a contract?
Is there a dispute between business partners or shareholders?
Are there any ongoing or threatened lawsuits against the business?
Do you have any compliance or regulatory concerns?
What is the approximate annual revenue of the business?
How many employees does the business have?
Are there any intellectual property assets — trademarks, patents, trade secrets?
Do you have any existing relationships with attorneys or legal counsel?
Are there any outstanding debts, loans, or investor obligations?
Do you need assistance with employment agreements or non-compete clauses?
Are you looking to raise capital or bring in investors?
Is the business involved in any government contracts or regulated activities?
What is your timeline for resolving this matter?
Who is the primary decision-maker or authorized representative?
What is the best way to contact you regarding this matter?
Is there anything else about your business situation the attorney should know?
Immigration: What is your full legal name as it appears on your passport?
What is your country of citizenship?
What is your current immigration status — visa holder, permanent resident, undocumented, or other?
When did you last enter the United States? What was the manner of entry?
What type of visa did you enter on, if applicable?
Has your visa expired? If so, when did it expire?
Have you ever been out of status or overstayed a visa?
What immigration benefit are you seeking — green card, citizenship, asylum, work permit, or visa?
Do you have a spouse or family member who is a U.S. citizen or permanent resident?
Have you ever filed a petition or application with USCIS? What was the result?
Have you ever been in removal or deportation proceedings?
Have you received any notices from immigration authorities — NTA, RFE, or denial?
Do you have any criminal history or arrests in the United States or abroad?
Are you currently employed? What is your employer's name and your job title?
Does your employer sponsor your visa or are they willing to?
Do you have any children? What are their ages and immigration statuses?
Have you ever been denied entry to the United States?
Have you ever applied for asylum or refugee status?
Do you have any pending court dates with the immigration court?
Are you in need of a work permit or travel document while your case is pending?
Do you have copies of your passport, visa, I-94, and any USCIS receipts?
Have you consulted with another immigration attorney about this matter?
Is there any urgency — upcoming deadline, court date, or expiring status?
What is the best phone number and email to reach you?
Is there anything else about your immigration situation the attorney should know?
Employment Law: What is your full legal name?
What is the name of your employer or former employer?
What is or was your job title and how long have you worked there?
Are you currently still employed at this company?
What is the nature of your employment issue — termination, discrimination, harassment, wage dispute, or retaliation?
When did the incident or issue first occur?
Can you describe what happened in your own words?
Who was involved — supervisor, coworker, HR, or management?
Do you believe you were treated differently because of your race, gender, age, religion, disability, or other protected characteristic?
Have you filed a complaint with HR or your company's internal process?
What was the company's response to your complaint?
Have you filed a charge with the EEOC or any state labor agency?
Do you have an employment contract, offer letter, or employee handbook?
Were you given any reason for your termination or adverse action?
Do you have any written documentation — emails, texts, performance reviews, or write-ups?
Were there any witnesses to the incidents you are describing?
Are there any non-compete or non-disclosure agreements in place?
Have you received your final paycheck and all owed compensation?
Are you owed any unpaid overtime, commissions, or bonuses?
Have you applied for unemployment benefits? Were they approved?
Have you consulted with or retained another attorney for this matter?
What outcome are you hoping for — reinstatement, compensation, or other?
Is there a statute of limitations concern we should be aware of?
What is the best way to contact you privately?
Is there anything else about your employment situation the attorney should know?
Bankruptcy: What is your full legal name and date of birth?
What is your current address?
Are you filing individually or jointly with a spouse?
What is your current employment status and occupation?
What is your approximate monthly income from all sources?
What is your approximate total amount of debt?
What types of debt do you have — credit cards, medical bills, student loans, mortgages, auto loans?
Are any of your debts secured — such as a mortgage or car loan?
Are you currently behind on any payments? Which ones?
Have you received any collection calls, letters, or lawsuits from creditors?
Is there a wage garnishment currently in effect?
Are you facing foreclosure on your home?
Has your car been repossessed or is it at risk of repossession?
Have you filed for bankruptcy before? If so, when and what chapter?
Are you interested in Chapter 7 liquidation or Chapter 13 reorganization?
Do you own any real estate? What is the approximate value and mortgage balance?
Do you have any significant assets — savings, investments, retirement accounts, or valuable property?
Have you transferred or sold any assets in the last two years?
Do you owe any taxes to the IRS or state tax authority?
Are there any pending lawsuits or judgments against you?
Do you have any cosigners on any of your debts?
Have you completed a credit counseling course?
Have you consulted with another attorney about bankruptcy?
What is the primary reason you are considering bankruptcy?
Is there anything else about your financial situation the attorney should know?
Estate Planning: What is your full legal name and date of birth?
What is your current marital status?
What is your spouse's or partner's full legal name?
Do you have children? What are their names and ages?
Do you have any grandchildren?
What type of estate planning document do you need — Will, Trust, Power of Attorney, Healthcare Directive, or all of the above?
Do you currently have any existing estate planning documents? When were they last updated?
Do you own any real estate? Please describe the properties.
What are your approximate total assets — including bank accounts, investments, retirement funds, and life insurance?
Do you own any business interests or partnerships?
Do you have any life insurance policies? What are the coverage amounts?
Do you have any retirement accounts — 401k, IRA, pension?
Who would you like to name as the primary beneficiary of your estate?
Who would you like to name as alternate or contingent beneficiaries?
Who would you like to serve as your Executor or Personal Representative?
Who would you like to serve as Trustee, if a trust is being created?
Who would you like to appoint as your Power of Attorney for financial matters?
Who would you like to appoint as your Healthcare Proxy or Agent?
Do you have any dependents with special needs that require a Special Needs Trust?
Are there any specific charitable organizations you would like to include?
Do you have any concerns about estate taxes or inheritance taxes?
Are there any family dynamics or situations that could complicate your estate plan?
Have you discussed your estate planning wishes with your family?
What is your timeline for completing these documents?
Is there anything else about your estate or wishes the attorney should know?
Intellectual Property: What is your full legal name or business name?
What type of intellectual property matter is this — patent, trademark, copyright, or trade secret?
Can you briefly describe the invention, brand, creative work, or proprietary information involved?
Have you already filed for any IP protection — patent application, trademark registration, or copyright registration?
If filed, what is the application or registration number?
When was the invention created, the brand first used, or the work first published?
Is this a new filing or are you dealing with an existing registration?
Are you seeking to protect your IP, enforce it against an infringer, or defend against a claim?
Have you received any cease and desist letters or infringement notices?
Are you aware of any third party using a similar mark, invention, or work?
Do you have documentation proving your ownership or creation date — prototypes, drafts, registration certificates?
Is the intellectual property being used commercially? If so, in what markets or territories?
Are there any licensing agreements in place for this IP?
Have you assigned or transferred any rights to another party?
Is there a co-inventor, co-author, or co-creator involved?
Do you have an existing relationship with a patent agent or IP attorney?
Are there any pending deadlines — office action responses, opposition periods, or renewal dates?
Is the IP associated with a business or startup? What stage is the business in?
Are there any international filing or protection needs?
Do you have a budget allocated for IP protection or enforcement?
Have you conducted any prior art searches or trademark searches?
What is your primary goal — registration, licensing, litigation, or portfolio management?
What industry or market does this IP relate to?
What is the best way to contact you regarding this matter?
Is there anything else about your IP situation the attorney should know?
